title: The Adventure of the Abbey Grange
synopsis: Inspector Hopkins summons Holmes to Abbey Grange in Kent, where the brutal Sir Eustace Brackenstall has been found battered to death and his wife Lady Mary tied to a chair. Lady Mary blames the Randall gang of burglars who came for her silver, but Holmes finds inconsistencies in her account — particularly the condition of the wine glasses.
solution: Captain Crocker, a sailor who loved Lady Mary and could not bear her abuse at the hands of her alcoholic husband, had visited the house and killed Sir Eustace in a fight; Lady Mary and Crocker staged the burglary scene; Holmes acts as a one-man jury and acquits Crocker, advising him to return in a year to claim Lady Mary.
notable_elements: Holmes notices that two of the three wine glasses have been drunk from, contradicting the story of three burglars
Features Holmes famously convening Watson as a jury and pronouncing a verdict outside the law
The victim Sir Eustace is presented as deserving of little sympathy — a brutal and drunken husband
Holmes's willingness to let a killer go free makes this one of the canon's most ethically complex resolutions
